limit_clause.label: LIMIT Clause
limit_clause.summary: Restricting the number of rows returned.

primary_sort.title: The first sort rule controls the main order of the result.
primary_sort.bodyMarkdown: When you sort by more than one column, the first column listed has the strongest control over the order.

secondary_sort.title: A second sort rule helps order rows that tie on the first one.
secondary_sort.bodyMarkdown: If two rows have the same value in the first sort column, the second sort column helps break the tie.

limit_results.title: LIMIT keeps only the most relevant rows when you do not need the full result.
limit_results.bodyMarkdown: ORDER BY and LIMIT are often used together to answer ranking-style questions like top values or newest rows.

practice_note.title: Practice by predicting the order first, then verifying it after running the query.
practice_note.bodyMarkdown: Say which rows should come first and why before you run the query.

module3_limit_clause_practice_sql.title: Practice: LIMIT Clause
module3_limit_clause_practice_sql.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the five most expensive products by sorting `price` from highest to lowest.

Then run it.

help.concept: Restricting the number of rows returned.
help.hint_2: LIMIT trims the output after sorting.

module3_limit_clause_practice_sql.hint: Use ORDER BY with DESC, then LIMIT 5.
module3_limit_clause_practice_sql.starterCode:
-- Show top priced rows
SELECT name, price
FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC
LIMIT 5

module3_limit_clause_core_concept.title: Core idea: LIMIT Clause
module3_limit_clause_core_concept.prompt: What is the best description of LIMIT Clause?

help.concept: Restricting the number of rows returned.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that matches the main concept directly.
help.hint_2: Ignore answers that describe unrelated SQL behavior.

module3_limit_clause_core_concept.hint: Match the answer to the topic summary.

options.a: It controls the order and size of the output so results are easier to read.
options.b: It permanently changes values inside the table.
options.c: It only works with grouped queries.
options.d: It deletes rows after ranking them.

module3_limit_clause_true_statements.title: True statements about LIMIT Clause
module3_limit_clause_true_statements.prompt: Select all true statements about LIMIT Clause.

help.concept: Restricting the number of rows returned.
help.hint_1: Pick statements that align with the topic summary and objectives.
help.hint_2: Reject statements that clearly describe unrelated SQL ideas.

module3_limit_clause_true_statements.hint: Use the summary and objectives as your guide.

options.a: ORDER BY changes the order of the result set.
options.b: LIMIT is often paired with ORDER BY.
options.c: A secondary sort can break ties.
options.d: Sorting always changes stored table data.
options.e: Descending order can help find highest values first.

module3_limit_clause_workflow_order.title: Workflow for LIMIT Clause
module3_limit_clause_workflow_order.prompt: Reorder the workflow ideas for LIMIT Clause.

help.concept: Choose the ordering rule, apply it, then trim the result if needed.
help.hint_1: Think about what should happen first.
help.hint_2: Then think about what should happen last.

tokens.t1: decide which rows should appear first
tokens.t3: apply LIMIT if only a few rows are needed

module3_limit_clause_keyword_fill.title: Sorting keyword
module3_limit_clause_keyword_fill.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best SQL keyword.

help.concept: Restricting the number of rows returned.
help.hint_1: Think about which SQL term best completes the sentence.
help.hint_2: Use the topic meaning to eliminate unrelated options.

module3_limit_clause_keyword_fill.hint: Match the sentence to the right SQL term.
module3_limit_clause_keyword_fill.template: The SQL clause used to sort query results is ____.
